SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from The Presiding Judge Department 206 for trial/no appearance by defendant (s).
Department 514, Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840. (514)

Bradley R. Chibos, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Charles H. Delacey.
No appearance on behalf of or by Defendant Rafael Alvarez.
Charles H. Delacey sworn and examined.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff Charles H. Delacey and against Defendant Rafael Alvarez as follows:
Principal $52,779.85
Pre-Judgment Interest $13,356.20
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $775.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $66,911.05
Judgment signed in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice And Motion For Continuance/Stay Of Hearing And Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Raphael Alvarez Reservation Nber: 02190321-10
Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAPHAEL ALVAREZ's Motion For Continuance/Stay Of Hearing And Proceedings.

Off calendar per request of the moving party. (email 3-15-19)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-01-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brad Chibos, Esq. 408-998-1694 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Rafael Alvarez, 561-562-3332 Defendant in Pro Per through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the basis for the tentative ruling; it is too late to contest the Court's jurisdiction and too late at this point to contest service. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RAPHAEL ALVAREZ's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE & CLAIM, AND TO STRIKE COMPLAINT AND DISMISS CASE.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Raphael Alvarez's motion to quash service of the summons and complaint, to strike the complaint, and to dismiss the case on the grounds of lack of jurisdiction and inconvenient forum is denied. Mr. Alvarez did not file a motion to quash service of summons on the ground of lack of jurisdiction of the court over him or a motion to stay or dismiss the action on the ground of inconvenient forum within the time allowed for filing a response to the complaint, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10(a). Instead, on November 9, 2018, he filed an answer to the complaint. He did not file the instant motion until December 21, 2018. Having failed to file a timely motion to quash, stay, or dismiss simultaneously with his answer, as allowed by Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10(e), defendant waived any objection to the court's exercise of personal jurisdiction by making a general appearance in the action. (Code Civ. Proc., 1014 ["A defendant appears in an action when the defendant answers . . . ."]; Roy v. Superior Court (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 337, 342.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
